items.original_candidate: (likely) Ron McKee or similar — Mark McGuire's buddy
items.evidence: Transcript context: Leventhal recounts playing American Legion baseball at age 15 in the South Bay (LA), where his teammates included 'the kids' of two dads who had played pro baseball — Jeff Pentland and Tom Griffin — and 'Ron McMacKinn is a buddy of Mark McGwire'. Jeff Pentland (b. 1946, Los Angeles, ASU/MLB pitching coach) is exactly Leventhal's age cohort and an established LA-area baseball figure. The original candidate ('Ron McKee') has no archival corroboration. The phonetically near-identical canonical spelling 'Ron McMackin' is attested as a documented California-baseball name on Baseball-Reference (Ronald McMackin, 1969 Kansas City Royals minor league — Winnipeg Goldeyes; also a Ron McMackin who is Chairman of Pan-Pacific Mechanical in Fountain Valley, CA). Whisper's rendering 'McMacKinn' is essentially the canonical 'McMackin' with a doubled letter — a typical Whisper orthographic artifact, not a substantive misidentification. I could not independently corroborate the specific Leventhal–McMackin–McGwire social-link claim in external sources, but the name 'Ron McMackin' is the only well-attested California-baseball Ron in the McMackin / McKee / McKeon / McMakin family of phonetic candidates. Retaining the speaker's pronunciation as 'Ron McMackin' (correcting only the orthography) is the least-invasive, most-defensible adjudication.

items.original_candidate: Cheryl LaBatts (or similar SCOPE veteran historian)
items.evidence: Transcript context: Leventhal mentions 'Sherry LeBetus who's written a new book, another woman who's writing a book, Maria Gitin University Alabama about scope, scope workers'. Maria Gitin is confirmed as the SCOPE veteran who wrote 'This Bright Light of Ours: Stories from the Voting Rights Fight' (University of Alabama Press, 2014). The SCOPE50 official book list (scope50.org/books, which catalogs SCOPE-related publications) confirms 'Labedis, Sherie Holbrook — You Came Here to Die, Didn't You (2011)' as the parallel SCOPE-veteran memoir. The Civil Rights Movement Veterans archive (crmvet.org/vet/labedis.htm) further confirms Sherie Labedis as a 1965 SCOPE volunteer who served in Pineville, South Carolina and authored that memoir. Whisper's 'Sherry LeBetus' is a clean phonetic rendering of 'Sherie Labedis' (LeBetus ≈ Labedis with the consonants softened). The original candidate 'Cheryl LaBatts' has no archival corroboration anywhere; 'Sherie Labedis' is the unambiguous canonical name.

items.original_candidate: (likely) Ron McKee / Ron McKeon (Mark McGwire associate)
items.evidence: Same Whisper rendering and same archival reasoning as row 129.86 (this row reinforces that earlier mention). The original candidate offered 'Ron McKee / Ron McKeon' as alternates, but neither has archival corroboration as a Mark McGwire associate. The single well-attested California-baseball 'Ron Mc-' name phonetically consistent with 'Ron McMacKinn' is 'Ron McMackin' (documented on Baseball-Reference for the 1969 KC Royals minor leaguer, and as the surname of the Pan-Pacific Mechanical chairman in Fountain Valley, CA). Whisper's doubled-K 'McMacKinn' is an orthographic artifact of the canonical 'McMackin'. The specific Ron-McMackin-as-McGwire-buddy social claim could not be independently sourced, but the name itself is the most defensible canonical rendering.
